methods.description: Process for collecting and chemically processing diatom frustules to extract silica nanoparticles for further analysis.

steps.description: Fresh samples were collected from the sampling site in the Wadi El-Rayan area at El-Fayum Governorate, Egypt.
steps.searchable_summary: collect fresh samples from Wadi El-Rayan area

steps.description: Fresh Diatom Frustules were rinsed with distilled water to remove the excess of salts adhering to them.
steps.searchable_summary: rinse diatom frustules with distilled water

steps.description: Silica Nanoparticles (SNPs) samples were extracted by chemically processing the fresh frustules with a strong H2SO4 and HNO3 mixture.
steps.searchable_summary: extract SNPs using H2SO4 and HNO3 mixture

steps.description: 5 mL of supernatant diatom frustules were mixed with 5 mL of a 2:1 ratio of concentrated H2SO4 and HNO3 (> 90%), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: mix diatom frustules with H2SO4 and HNO3

steps.description: The mixed solution of strong acids and diatoms was stirred at 1000 rpm, keeping the temperature at nearly 90 ºC for 3 h.
steps.searchable_summary: stir solution at 1000 rpm and 90 ºC for 3 hours

steps.description: Emulsions of the fresh and chemically processed samples were prepared by dispersing 0.02 g of each sample powder in 10 mL of ethylene glycol.
steps.searchable_summary: disperse sample powder in ethylene glycol

methods.description: Techniques used to analyze the structural and chemical properties of the samples using TEM, XPS, and optical absorption spectroscopy.

steps.description: TEM images and electron diffraction were recorded using a JEOL® JEM-2100LaB6 apparatus operating at 120 kV.
steps.searchable_summary: record TEM images and electron diffraction

steps.description: XPS was used to investigate the chemical configuration of the different atomic species in both samples.
The spectra were scanned for each finely powdered sample spread on carbon tapes under 10 − 10 mbar of UHV conditions.
steps.searchable_summary: scan XPS spectra under UHV conditions

steps.description: Optical absorption spectra, in the 190-900 nm wavelength range, were collected at room temperature with a Perkin-Elmer Lambda 25/35 double-beam spectrophotometer.

methods.description: Procedure for measuring the electrical properties of the samples using spin-coating and I-V curve analysis.

steps.description: Two droplets of each sample emulsion were deposited on top of the p-type Si(111) wafer surface using the spin-coating technique.
steps.searchable_summary: deposit sample emulsion on Si wafer using spin-coating

steps.description: Samples were left to dry after spin-coating.
Copper (Cu) wires were used as the ohmic metallic contacts and the I-V curves were measured using a Keithley electrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: measure I-V curves using Keithley electrometer
